## Variable syntax changed: `{{name}}` instead of `:name:` ⚠️

A small but **breaking** tweak to the batch-print variable syntax introduced in 1.6.0. Placeholders now use double curly braces:

```
old: Hello :name:
new: Hello {{name}}
```

### Why

Colons are common in legitimate QR / barcode content — URLs (`http://`), IPv6 literals, port numbers, `key:value` pairs, base64 padding — and the old `:name:` regex was greedy enough to false-match them. Switching to `{{ }}` brings labelle-web in line with Handlebars / Jinja / Mustache and drops the collision risk to near zero.

### What to do

Re-save any label `.json` files that contain `:varname:` placeholders, replacing them with `{{varname}}`. If you don't, those placeholders will print literally instead of being substituted.

(If you haven't saved any label files yet — which is most users, since batch mode just shipped two days ago — no action needed.)

### Anything else?

No behavioural changes. Auto-detection, the variable-rename heuristic, cut marks, copies, pause, cancel — all unchanged. Just the placeholder syntax.

---
